title: Unauthenticated swap callback lets anyone force WETH transfers out of the contract
reason: This is only true for the local exploit harness contract (`DRLVaultV3_EXP`), not the target vault at `VAULT_ADDR`; draining the attacker PoC is not a protocol finding.

title: Anyone can trigger the full flash-loan trading sequence against contract-held balances
reason: `testExploit()` is part of the PoC harness rather than the target protocol. The only reportable part is the vault-side permissionless/slippage abuse, which is folded into F-002 instead of kept as a separate issue.

title: Unlimited token allowances are granted to multiple external contracts and never revoked
reason: The approvals are set by the exploit harness for its own execution path. They do not describe a confirmed unsafe approval pattern inside the target vault contract.

title: Flash-loan callback trusts the caller address only and ignores callback parameters
reason: This concerns the PoC receiver contract, not the target vault. Missing extra callback validation here does not create independent protocol harm beyond the intentionally scripted exploit flow.
